{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Krönungsfest |NAME = W3:I9-F35-N35-N35:V28-G21-D58-D28:D28:D28-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau
''{{unicode|ḥbf-nfr-n-Nḥb-k3w}}'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Schönes Fest der Krönung'' }} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''Krönungsfest''' (auch '''Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau''') ist seit dem [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleren Reich]] belegt und dauerte vom ersten bis zum zweiten Tag des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|Peret]]. == Hintergrund == === Festverlauf === Der [[Totengott]] [[Neheb-kau]] fungierte in der [[Duat]] als einer der Richter beim [[Totengericht]] und bewertete die Chraktereigenschaften der [[Egoismus|Selbstsucht]] und des [[Hochmut]]s. Bei den Zeremonien des Krönungsfestes übernahm Neheb-kau die Rolle des Helfers und verlieh in Anerkennung der vollbrachten Leistungen jeweils nach Ablauf eines Jahres erneut die Krone des Herrschers. Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun|Amun-Re]] und damit verbunden seiner Funktion als Götterkönig. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle des Amun-Re. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Amun-Re selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] die Krone übertragen bekam. === Verkündungsfest === Das Krönungsfest hatte zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere Jubiläen des Königs ausrief, beispielsweise das [[Sedfest|Sed-Fest]], das die Ägypter im [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reich]] am festgetzten Termin des [[Schemu|27. Schemu II]] ([[Payni]]) feierten. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von [[Ramses II.]] aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun|Amun-Re]] und damit verbunden seiner Funktion als Götterkönig. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle des Amun-Re. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Amun-Re selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] die Krone übertragen bekam. === Verkündungsfest === Das Krönungsfest hatte zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere Jubiläen des Königs ausrief, beispielsweise das [[Sedfest|Sed-Fest]], das die Ägypter im [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reich]] am festgetzten Termin des [[Schemu|27. Schemu II]] ([[Payni]]) feierten. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von [[Ramses II.]] aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Krönungsfest |NAME = W3:I9-F35-N35-N35:V28-G21-D58-D28:D28:D28-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau
''{{unicode|ḥbf-nfr-n-Nḥb-k3w}}'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Schönes Fest der Krönung'' }} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''Krönungsfest''' (auch '''Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau''') ist seit dem [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleren Reich]] belegt und dauerte vom ersten bis zum zweiten Tag des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|Peret]] ([[Tybi]]). == Hintergrund == === Festverlauf === Der [[Totengott]] [[Neheb-kau]] fungierte in der [[Duat]] als einer der Richter beim [[Totengericht]] und bewertete die Charaktereigenschaften der [[Egoismus|Selbstsucht]] und des [[Hochmut]]s. Bei den Zeremonien des Krönungsfestes übernahm Neheb-kau die Rolle des Helfers und verlieh in Anerkennung der vollbrachten Leistungen jeweils nach Ablauf eines Jahres erneut die Krone des Herrschers. Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun|Amun-Re]] und damit verbunden seiner Funktion als Götterkönig. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle des Amun-Re. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Amun-Re selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] die Krone übertragen bekam. [[Ramses III.]] ersetzte das Krönungsfest durch eine Opferliste für das Fest vom 28. Peret I; zeitgleich wurde daneben das [[Horus-Fest]] im Monat [[Ka-her-ka]] zelebriert, das jedoch zunächst im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den ersten Tag der Jahreszeit Peret angesetzt war, was zeitweilig zu Verlegungen des Krönungsfestes führte. === Verkündungsfest === Das Krönungsfest hatte zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere Jubiläen des Königs ausrief, beispielsweise das [[Sedfest|Sed-Fest]], das die Ägypter im [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reich]] am festgetzten Termin des [[Schemu|27. Schemu II]] ([[Payni]]) feierten. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von [[Ramses II.]] aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Krönungsfest |NAME = W3:I9-F35-N35-N35:V28-G21-D58-D28:D28:D28-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau
''{{unicode|ḥbf-nfr-n-Nḥb-k3w}}'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Schönes Fest mit dem (des) Gott(es) Neheb-kau /
Schönes Fest der Krönung'' }} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''Krönungsfest''' (auch '''Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau''') ist seit dem [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleren Reich]] belegt und dauerte vom ersten bis zum zweiten Tag des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|Peret]] ([[Tybi]]). == Hintergrund == === Festverlauf === Der [[Totengott]] [[Neheb-kau]] fungierte in der [[Duat]] als einer der Richter beim [[Totengericht]] und bewertete die Charaktereigenschaften der [[Egoismus|Selbstsucht]] und des [[Hochmut]]s. Bei den [[Zeremonie]]n des Krönungsfestes übernahm Neheb-kau die Rolle des Helfers und verlieh in Anerkennung der vollbrachten Leistungen jeweils nach Ablauf eines Jahres erneut die Krone des Herrschers. Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun|Amun-Re]] und damit verbunden seiner Funktion als Götterkönig. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle des Amun-Re. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Amun-Re selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] die Krone übertragen bekam. [[Ramses III.]] ersetzte das Krönungsfest anlässlich eines Sieges über die [[Mešweš|Meschwesch]] vom 28. Peret I durch eine Opferliste; zeitgleich wurde daneben das [[Horus-Fest]] im Monat [[Ka-her-ka]] zelebriert, das jedoch zunächst im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den ersten Tag der Jahreszeit Peret angesetzt war und deshalb zeitweilig zu Verlegungen des Krönungsfestes führte. === Verkündungsfest === Das Krönungsfest hatte zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere [[Jubiläum|Jubiläen]] des Königs ausrief, beispielsweise das [[Sedfest|Sed-Fest]], das die Ägypter im [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reich]] am festgesetzten Termin des [[Schemu|27. Schemu II]] ([[Payni]]) feierten. Den Hintergrund der Terminfestsetzung bildete das [[Talfest]], da es im ungünstigsten Fall durch die Ansetzung im Mondkalender auf den 26. Schemu II fallen konnte. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von [[Ramses II.]] aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = W3:I9-F35-N35-N35:V28-G21-D58-D28:D28:D28-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau
''{{unicode|ḥbf-nfr-n-Nḥb-k3w}}'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Schönes Fest mit dem (des) Gott(es) Neheb-kau'' }} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''[[Neheb-kau]]-Fest''' (auch '''Heb-nefer-en-Neheb-kau''') ist seit dem [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleren Reich]] belegt und dauerte vom ersten bis zum zweiten Tag des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|Peret]] ([[Tybi]]). == Hintergrund == === Festverlauf === Der [[Totengott]] Neheb-kau fungierte in der [[Duat]] als einer der Richter beim [[Totengericht]] und bewertete die Charaktereigenschaften der [[Egoismus|Selbstsucht]] und des [[Hochmut]]s. Bei den [[Zeremonie]]n des Krönungsfestes übernahm Neheb-kau die Rolle des Helfers und verlieh in Anerkennung der vollbrachten Leistungen jeweils nach Ablauf eines Jahres erneut die Krone des Herrschers. Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun|Amun-Re]] und damit verbunden seiner Funktion als Götterkönig. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle des Amun-Re. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Amun-Re selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] die Krone übertragen bekam. === Verkündungsfest unter Ramses II. === Das Neheb-Kau-Fest hatte unter [[Ramses II.]] zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere [[Jubiläum|Jubiläen]] des Königs ausrief. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von Ramses II. aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''{{unicode|ḥb-Nḥb-k3w}}'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Fest des Neheb-kau'' }} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''[[Neheb-kau]]-Fest''' (auch '''Horus-Fest''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] als göttliches sowie königliches „Erneuerungsfest“ belegt und dauerte vom ersten bis zum zweiten Tag des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|Peret]] ([[Tybi]]); zugleich fungierte es als „Neujahrsfest des [[Horus]]“. == Hintergrund == === Festverlauf === Der [[Totengott]] Neheb-kau fungierte in der [[Duat]] als einer der Richter beim [[Totengericht]] und bewertete die Charaktereigenschaften der [[Egoismus|Selbstsucht]] und des [[Hochmut]]s. Bei den [[Zeremonie]]n übernahm Neheb-kau die Rolle des Helfers und verlieh in Anerkennung der vollbrachten Leistungen jeweils nach Ablauf eines Jahres erneut die Götterkrone des Herrschers. Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun-Re]], in [[Edfu]] Horus als Sohn des [[Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Neheb-kau selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] bestätigt wurde. Von [[Ramses III.]] ist ein Festeintrag erhalten geblieben: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, Neujahrstag des Horus: Tag des Erscheinens des Königs Horus von Edfu, des Sohnes von Re, den die Menschen lieben. Man soll jede Zeremonie vollziehen wie am [[Achet#Datierungen|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Verkündungsfest unter Ramses II. === Das Neheb-Kau-Fest hatte unter [[Ramses II.]] zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere [[Jubiläum|Jubiläen]] des Königs ausrief. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von Ramses II. aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Neheb-kau selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] bestätigt wurde. Von [[Ramses III.]] ist ein Festeintrag erhalten geblieben: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, Neujahrstag des Horus: Tag des Erscheinens des Königs Horus von Edfu, des Sohnes von Re, den die Menschen lieben. Man soll jede Zeremonie vollziehen wie am [[Achet#Datierungen|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Verkündungsfest unter Ramses II. === Das Neheb-Kau-Fest hatte unter [[Ramses II.]] zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König während der Feierlichkeiten besondere [[Jubiläum|Jubiläen]] des Königs ausrief. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von Ramses II. aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die Sedfeste 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. revision.comment: Sat Ra verschob Seite [[Neheb-kau-Fest]] nach [[Nehebkau-Fest]]: mit Bezug auf den Artikel [[Nehebkau]] besser so

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Fest des Neheb-kau'' }} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''[[Nehebkau]]-Fest''' (auch '''[[Choiak#Fest der Isis-Riten|Horus-Fest]]''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] als göttliches sowie königliches „Erneuerungsfest“ belegt und dauerte vom ersten bis zum zweiten Tag des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|Peret]] ([[Tybi]]); zugleich fungierte es als „Neujahrsfest des [[Horus]]“. == Hintergrund == === Festverlauf === Der [[Totengott]] [[Nehebkau]] fungierte in der [[Duat]] als einer der Richter beim [[Totengericht]] und bewertete die Charaktereigenschaften der [[Egoismus|Selbstsucht]] und des [[Hochmut]]s. Bei den [[Zeremonie]]n übernahm Neheb-kau die Rolle des Helfers und verlieh in Anerkennung der vollbrachten Leistungen jeweils nach Ablauf eines Jahres erneut die Götterkrone des Herrschers. Der erste Tag galt der Gottheit [[Amun-Re]], in [[Edfu]] Horus als Sohn des [[Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]. Während der Fahrt des Amun-Re in seiner [[Barke|Sonnenbarke]] erneuerte Neheb-kau die göttliche Herrscherrolle. Am Morgen des zweiten Tages nahm die Bevölkerung beim [[Prozession]]szug des Königs teil, der nun von Neheb-kau selbst [[Ritual|rituell]] bestätigt wurde. Von [[Ramses III.]] ist ein Festeintrag erhalten geblieben: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, Neujahrstag des Horus: Tag des Erscheinens des Königs Horus von Edfu, des Sohnes von Re, den die Menschen lieben. Man soll jede Zeremonie vollziehen wie am [[Achet#Datierungen|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Verkündungsfest unter Ramses II. === Das Nehebkau-Fest hatte unter [[Ramses II.]] zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König ([[Pharao]]) während der Feierlichkeiten besondere [[Jubiläum|Jubiläen]] des Königs ausrief. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von Ramses II. aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die [[Sedfest]]e 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse'' 1950, 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau

Man soll jede Zeremonie vollziehen wie am [[Achet#Datierungen|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Verkündungsfest unter Ramses II. === Das Nehebkau-Fest hatte unter [[Ramses II.]] zugleich den Charakter eines Verkündungsfestes, da der jeweilige König ([[Pharao]]) während der Feierlichkeiten besondere [[Jubiläum|Jubiläen]] des Königs ausrief. Gut belegt sind die Verkündungen von Ramses II. aus den Regierungsjahren 42 , 45 , 51 , 54 , 63  und 66  für die Sedfeste 5, 6, 8, 9, 12 und 13. Abweichend erfolgte im 57. und 60. Regierungsjahr die Verkündung für die [[Sedfest]]e 10 und 11 am 17. Peret II mit fünfzehntägiger Verspätung. == Literatur == * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse'' 1950, 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Alten Reich}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Alten Reich}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

revision.comment: moving the navbar to the bottom and removing duplicates

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Alten Reich}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Alten Reich}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Alten Reich}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]][[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|12=draft}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|12=draft}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=draft}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom|12=draft}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=draft}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=draft}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|12=draft}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat|12=draft}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判|12=draft}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht|12=draft}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|12=draft}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|12=draft}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=draft}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|12=draft}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|12=draft}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|12=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|12=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|12=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|12=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|12=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]]

During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|12=draft|12=draft}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|12=draft|12=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|12=draft|12=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|12=draft|12=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|12=draft|12=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|12=draft|12=draft}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|12=draft|12=draft}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom|12=draft|12=draft}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=draft|12=draft}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft|12=draft}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|12=draft|12=draft}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判|12=draft|12=draft}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|12=draft|12=draft}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|12=draft|12=draft}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|12=draft|12=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|12=draft|12=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|12=draft|12=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|12=draft|12=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]]

Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|12=draft|12=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|12=draft|12=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|12=draft|12=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|12=draft|12=draft}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|12=draft|12=draft}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom|12=draft|12=draft}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=draft|12=draft}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft|12=draft}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|12=draft|12=draft}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判|12=draft|12=draft}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|12=draft|12=draft}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|12=draft|12=draft}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|12=draft|12=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|12=draft|12=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|12=draft|12=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|12=draft|12=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|12=draft|12=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|12=draft|12=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest|12=draft]]

'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]]

Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}}

'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}}
