'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}}

'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[category:Completion tier 1]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}}

During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=draft}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|12=draft}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|12=draft}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|12=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|12=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|12=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|12=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|12=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|qq=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|qq=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|qq=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|qq=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|qq=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|qq=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|qq=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.text:
'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|qq=draft}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|qq=draft}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom|qq=draft}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|qq=draft}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|qq=draft}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat|qq=draft}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判|qq=draft}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=draft}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|qq=draft}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|qq=draft}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|qq=draft}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|qq=draft}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|qq=draft}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|qq=draft}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|qq=draft}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|qq=draft}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|qq=draft}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|qq=draft}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|qq=draft}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremony|qq=}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edfu|qq=}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Procession|qq=}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual|qq=}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramses III|qq=}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1. Achet I|qq=}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramses II|qq=}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharaoh|qq=}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jubilees|qq=}} of the king during the celebrations. '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau|qq=}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau|qq=}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Judgment of the Dead|qq=}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selfishness|qq=}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremony|qq=}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edfu|qq=}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|de|Prozession|ja|行列|zh|游行|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Procession|qq=}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|ritual|de|Ritual|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|lt=rituell|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual|qq=}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramses III|qq=}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|1. Achet I|de|Achet|ja|アシェト I|zh|阿切特 I|lt=1. Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1. Achet I|qq=}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramses II|qq=}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharaoh|qq=}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jubilees|qq=}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed Festival|qq=}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed Festival|de|Sedfest|ja|セド祭|zh|塞德节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sed Festival|qq=}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau|qq=}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich|ja|古王国|zh|古王国|lt=Old Kingdom|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=}} {{ill|Nehebkau|de|Nehebkau|ja|ネヘブカウ|zh|内赫布考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau|qq=}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|Duat|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|死者の裁き|zh|死者审判|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Judgment of the Dead|qq=}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Selfishness|de|Egoismus|ja|エゴイズム|zh|利己主义|lt=Selbstsucht|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selfishness|qq=}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|de|Zeremonie|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremony|qq=}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, in {{ill|Edfu|de|Edfu|ja|エドフ|zh|埃德富|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edfu|qq=}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. A festival record from {{ill|Ramses III|de|Ramses III.|ja|ラムセス3世|zh|拉美西斯三世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramses III|qq=}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Achet I|qq=}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramses II|de|Ramses II.|ja|ラムセス2世|zh|拉美西斯二世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramses II|qq=}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|zh|法老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharaoh|qq=}}) proclaimed special {{ill|jubilees|de|Jubiläum|ja|記念祭|zh|庆典|lt=Jubiläen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jubilees|qq=}} of the king during the celebrations. '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|lt=altägyptische|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|lt=Re|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[en:Nehebkau Festival]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

revision.text:
'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:22, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Nehebkau Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] }} [[sn:Nehebkau Festival]] [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[sn: failed]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1974477}}

[[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1974477]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:22, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1974477}}

''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1974477}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1974477}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
