Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1974477}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1974477]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:22, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The [[:en:Ancient Egypt|altägyptische]] '''[[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]]-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Nehebkau|Nehebkau]] acted in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as one of the judges in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] and evaluated the character traits of [[:en:Egoism|Selbstsucht]] and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the [[:en:Ceremony|Ceremony]], Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in [[:en:Edfu|Edfu]] to Horus as the son of [[:en:Ra|Re]]. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the [[:en:Procession|Procession]] of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself [[:en:Ritual|rituell]]. A festival record from [[:en:Ramesses III|Ramses III]] has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|1. Achet I]].|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramses II]] also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) proclaimed special [[:en:Jubilee|Jubiläen]] of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1974477}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. revision.text:
[[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] '''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=altägyptische}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|en|Nehebkau|WD=Q2044276|lt=Nehebkau}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Nehebkau|en|Nehebkau|WD=Q2044276|lt=Nehebkau}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgement (afterlife)|en|Judgement (afterlife)|WD=Q1236322|lt=Weighing of the Heart}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Egoism|en|Egoism|WD=Q238350|lt=Selbstsucht}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|en|Ceremony|WD=Q2627975|lt=Ceremony}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|WD=Q115665045|lt=Procession}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramses III}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=1. Achet I}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramses II}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh}}) proclaimed special {{ill|Jubilee|en|Jubilee|WD=Q15955568|lt=Jubiläen}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1974477}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|WD=Q115665045|lt=Procession}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=rituell}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramses III}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:ネヘブカウ祭]] [[de:Nehebkau-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1974477}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=altägyptische|1=Ancient Egypt}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|en|Nehebkau|WD=Q2044276|lt=Nehebkau|1=Nehebkau}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Nehebkau|en|Nehebkau|WD=Q2044276|lt=Nehebkau|1=Nehebkau}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgement (afterlife)|en|Judgement (afterlife)|WD=Q1236322|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Judgement (afterlife)}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Egoism|en|Egoism|WD=Q238350|lt=Selbstsucht|1=Egoism}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re|1=Ra}}. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|WD=Q115665045|lt=Procession|1=Procession}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=rituell|1=Ritual}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramses III|1=Ramesses III}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. revision.text:
'''Nehebkau Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-G21-V28-D58-Z9-D28:Z2-D30 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-Neheb-kau
''ḥb-Nḥb-k3w'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival of Neheb-kau'' }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=altägyptische|1=Ancient Egypt}} '''{{ill|Nehebkau|en|Nehebkau|WD=Q2044276|lt=Nehebkau|1=Nehebkau}}-Festival''' (also known as '''{{ill|Horus Festival|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|zh|荷鲁斯节|lt=Horus-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}} as a divine and royal "renewal festival" and lasted from the first to the second day of the first month of the season {{ill|Peret|de|Peret|ja|ペレト|zh|佩雷特|lt=Peret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tybi|de|Tybi|ja|ティビ|zh|提比|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}); at the same time, it functioned as the "New Year Festival of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}". == Background == === Festival Course === The {{ill|God of the Dead|de|Totengott|ja|死者の神|zh|死神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/God of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Nehebkau|en|Nehebkau|WD=Q2044276|lt=Nehebkau|1=Nehebkau}} acted in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}} as one of the judges in the {{ill|Judgement (afterlife)|en|Judgement (afterlife)|WD=Q1236322|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Judgement (afterlife)}} and evaluated the character traits of {{ill|Egoism|en|Egoism|WD=Q238350|lt=Selbstsucht|1=Egoism}} and {{ill|Arrogance|de|Hochmut|ja|高慢|zh|傲慢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arrogance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Ceremony|en|Ceremony|WD=Q2627975|lt=Ceremony|1=Ceremony}}, Nehebkau assumed the role of the assistant and, in recognition of the achievements, once again bestowed the divine crown of the ruler after the completion of a year. The first day was dedicated to the deity {{ill|Amun-Ra|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アモン・ラー|zh|阿蒙-拉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|WD=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}} to Horus as the son of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re|1=Ra}}. During the voyage of Amun-Ra in his {{ill|Solar Bark|de|Barke|ja|太陽の舟|zh|太阳之舟|lt=Sonnenbarke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Bark|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nehebkau renewed the divine rulership. On the morning of the second day, the populace participated in the {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|WD=Q115665045|lt=Procession|1=Procession}} of the king, who was now ritually confirmed by Nehebkau himself {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=rituell|1=Ritual}}. A festival record from {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramses III|1=Ramesses III}} has been preserved: {{Zitat|1. Peret I, New Year’s Day of Horus: The day of the appearance of King Horus of Edfu, the son of Ra, beloved by the people. Every ceremony should be performed as on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=1. Achet I|1=Season of the Inundation}}.|Edfou V, 351, 3–4}} === Proclamation Festival under Ramses II === The Nehebkau Festival under {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramses II|1=Ramesses II}} also had the character of a proclamation festival, as the reigning king ({{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh|1=Pharaoh}}) proclaimed special {{ill|Jubilee|en|Jubilee|WD=Q15955568|lt=Jubiläen|1=Jubilee}} of the king during the celebrations. Well-documented are the proclamations of Ramses II from the regnal years 42, 45, 51, 54, 63, and 66 for the {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival|1=Sed festival}} numbers 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, and 13. Deviating from this, in the 57th and 60th regnal years, the proclamation for {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival|1=Sed festival}} numbers 10 and 11 was made on 17 Peret II with a fifteen‐day delay. == Literature == * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, among others, Mainz, 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Nehebkau-Fest|version=201349561|comment=Imported full de history}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1974477|de|Nehebkau-Fest|ja|ネヘブカウ祭}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
